jokes.text: It's a broom, Bender. Today's a big day, and I want the place to look nice for Leela.
jokes.setup: Bender asks what contraption Fry is using
jokes.punchline: It's just a broom - Fry is so slovenly that basic cleaning tools are foreign to Bender
jokes.explanation: Good character work - establishes their slovenly lifestyle while showing Fry's growth motivation

jokes.text: Ah, just like the loverly black snow of old London.
jokes.punchline: Bender romanticizes the filth as nostalgic pollution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - romantic nostalgia for something gross and industrial

jokes.text: Fry's stammering breakdown trying to express feelings
jokes.setup: Fry wants to show maturity to Leela
jokes.punchline: He completely breaks down mid-sentence and gives up on emotional growth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry characterization - his emotional immaturity derailing his attempt at maturity

jokes.text: Hey, that's the vase I keep my cabbages in!
jokes.setup: Cabbage fight has apparently broken something
jokes.punchline: They use decorative items for vegetable storage
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the cabbage absurdity into their living situation

jokes.text: Oh ho! Let the judgmenting begin.
jokes.setup: Leela asking practical questions about furniture
jokes.punchline: Bender's made-up word 'judgmenting' and his defensive attitude
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - defensive, creates his own words, anticipates criticism

jokes.text: That tiny closet is my room!
jokes.setup: Leela suggests putting things in tiny closet
jokes.punchline: The closet IS Bender's entire room
jokes.explanation: Good reveal joke with perfect economy - shows how cramped their living situation is

jokes.text: Oh yeah. They have all the top slightly misspelled brands.
jokes.punchline: Their selling point is knockoff brands with spelling errors
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of knockoff e-commerce - specific and relatable observation

jokes.text: No wait! There's free shipping on a thousand.
jokes.punchline: Falls into the classic free shipping trap of ordering way too much
jokes.explanation: Relatable e-commerce satire - everyone's fallen for this psychological trick

jokes.text: Wow. Fast delivery.
jokes.punchline: They arrive instantly in massive quantity
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about instant delivery - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Right. Don't wanna get mud on the cabbages.
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting shoe caddy for cleanliness
jokes.punchline: Fry's priority is protecting their floor cabbages
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows their warped priorities and living conditions

jokes.text: Hey! You aren't the only ones who live here!
jokes.setup: Domestic scene with Fry and Leela
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's sudden appearance reminds us he exists and lives there too
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg - forgotten, neglected, making his presence known awkwardly

jokes.text: I can take a hint! But what I can't take is a straightforward request.
jokes.punchline: Bender's backwards logic about hints vs. direct requests
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - contrarian, illogical, but with consistent internal logic. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: But now, I'm like the third wheel on a tricycle that used to have two wheels!
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about feeling left out
jokes.punchline: Mangled metaphor - tricycles always have three wheels
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bender's emotional confusion expressed through mangled metaphors

jokes.text: That's a bicycle. - You're a bicycle!
jokes.punchline: Bender's childish comeback using bicycle as an insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect childish Bender response - uses the correction as a nonsensical insult

jokes.text: Yes, yes, the company cares deeply about your insert problem here.
jokes.punchline: Professor's form-letter response with literal template language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke - shows corporate indifference through literal template language

jokes.text: We can barely even afford these single-use pointin' sticks!
jokes.punchline: Wasteful single-use pointing devices as a budget concern
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist gag about corporate waste and environmental concerns

jokes.text: What kind of crap? - Bull.
jokes.setup: Asking what kind of crap they're delivering
jokes.punchline: Literal bull crap/manure
jokes.explanation: Simple pun with good structure but predictable - works as setup for moon setting

jokes.text: We know you have cheaper, faster, better options.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging they're inferior to competitors
jokes.explanation: Good corporate satire - honest about their inadequacy in typical corporate-speak format

jokes.text: Their warehouse is ruining the moon's natural ugliness.
jokes.punchline: Wanting to preserve the moon's ugliness as a natural feature
jokes.explanation: Excellent environmental satire - subverts expectations about what's worth preserving

jokes.text: If you'd care to join us, y'all'd fit in right natural with a room full of toothless rubes.
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecatingly describes his own group as toothless rubes
jokes.explanation: Good character work - self-aware rural stereotype who owns his limitations

jokes.text: There'll be 300 of us and 18 teeth.
jokes.setup: Following up on toothless rubes comment
jokes.punchline: Specific math showing extreme dental problems
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with specific absurd math - makes the rural stereotype more vivid

jokes.text: Don't worry, Pa! We done took jobs in the warehouse! You've been an amazing dad.
jokes.punchline: Daughters got jobs at the place destroying his farm, delivered as good news with gratitude
jokes.explanation: Dark economic satire - children working for the system destroying their family business

jokes.text: Huh. Not quite sure how she got in here.
jokes.punchline: Casual confusion about how robot got into anti-corporate meeting
jokes.explanation: Mild beat - acknowledges the absurdity without making a big deal of it

jokes.text: Why don't you go to hell and be a part of that community?
jokes.setup: Mom talking about being part of community
jokes.punchline: Farmer suggesting hell as her community with same phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure using Mom's own phrasing against her - satisfying hostility

jokes.text: Financial remuneration. - That's just a bribe. - It is? Hear her out!
jokes.punchline: When called a bribe, farmer suddenly becomes interested
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing corruption - they're against bribes until they realize they're being offered one

jokes.text: Always listening... Always watching... But also respecting your privacy because privacy comes first.
jokes.punchline: Contradicts surveillance description with privacy claim
jokes.explanation: Sharp tech industry satire - perfectly captures contradictory privacy claims

jokes.text: Goshdarn it, I never could stay mad at no one what gives me free stuff.
jokes.punchline: Admits he's easily bought with free items
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - shows how easily principles are abandoned for freebies

jokes.text: That warehouse is hiding nightmares beyond horror. Love the return policy though.
jokes.setup: Leela expressing concerns about warehouse conditions
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts horror with praise for customer service
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition joke - captures consumer cognitive dissonance perfectly. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: Ow! - Slap recognized.
jokes.punchline: Mom's slap is her biometric identifier instead of palm print
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific gag - Mom's signature move becomes her security system. Perfect integration.

jokes.text: Yes. They're much better than unions. I-I mean humans.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals anti-union sentiment, corrects to anti-human
jokes.explanation: Sharp labor satire - reveals corporate priorities through Freudian slip structure

jokes.text: This way to the exit. Thank you for visiting Momazon. And stay out!
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile addition
jokes.explanation: Good Bender characterization - can't maintain customer service facade

jokes.text: Plus, they let you pee in a bottle.
jokes.punchline: Amazon worker condition presented as a perk for a robot who doesn't pee
jokes.explanation: Sharp labor satire - horrible working conditions presented as benefits, plus robot logic

jokes.text: Since when do you need to pee? - I don't need to.
jokes.punchline: He admits he doesn't need to but still sees it as a benefit
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that makes the absurdity explicit - Bender's illogical priorities

jokes.text: Here are some grandfather Crocs.
jokes.punchline: AI mishears and offers grandfather-themed footwear
jokes.explanation: Decent AI misunderstanding joke - plays on voice recognition errors

jokes.text: Here is a grandfather box. Order within six minutes for next-day burial.
jokes.punchline: Offers coffin with burial service urgency
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation with good e-commerce urgency parody - morbid but well-structured

jokes.text: Decorative lamp. - No... Well, actually, we could use a lamp. Maybe this one. - Maybe this one. Maybe both.
jokes.punchline: Consumers getting drawn into buying things they don't need
jokes.explanation: Good consumer behavior satire - shows how easily we're manipulated into purchases

jokes.text: Crazy-ass box. Got it.
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual acceptance of wasteful practice
jokes.explanation: Good Bender response - doesn't question absurd corporate practices

jokes.text: Diarrhea medicine? Better pack this one with extra tape for privacy.
jokes.punchline: His solution is extra tape instead of discrete packaging
jokes.explanation: Good Bender logic - misunderstands privacy and makes things worse

jokes.text: And the Department of Commerce also went out of business.
jokes.setup: Reporting on businesses going out of business
jokes.punchline: Even the government department tracking it went out of business
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation joke with perfect economy - shows complete economic collapse through recursive absurdity

jokes.text: where people pay a fortune for free shipping, and also get TV shows for some reason.
jokes.punchline: Points out the illogical bundling of shipping and entertainment
jokes.explanation: Sharp Amazon Prime satire - captures the absurd bundling model perfectly

jokes.text: Shut up! I was talking to Invasa.
jokes.punchline: Was actually talking to the AI the whole time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mom characterization - treats AI better than her own sons, great misdirection

jokes.text: Slap balm? Why would we need... Ow!
jokes.setup: AI predicts need for slap balm
jokes.punchline: Mom immediately slaps, proving the prediction right
jokes.explanation: Brilliant predictive AI joke with perfect timing - sets up and pays off immediately with character consistency

jokes.text: Who's a smart speaker? - Is it me? Yep. Yep. Nope. Aw yeah! Look at me go!
jokes.setup: Mom praising AI like a pet
jokes.punchline: AI responds with childlike enthusiasm and self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Good AI personality development - shows it has childlike ego and need for validation

jokes.text: Debauchery, sir, in the middle of a shift? Heaven forfend!
jokes.setup: Bender suggesting drinking on the job
jokes.punchline: Hedonismbot, who lives for excess, is shocked by workplace drinking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character reversal - Hedonismbot having work ethics while Bender doesn't. Perfect irony.

jokes.text: But, Bender, you are home.
jokes.punchline: Mom reveals workers live at the warehouse permanently
jokes.explanation: Chilling labor dystopia reveal with perfect economy - shows the horror of company towns

jokes.text: There's no place like work.
jokes.punchline: Corrupted version replacing 'home' with 'work'
jokes.explanation: Effective dystopian brainwashing reference - corrupts beloved quote for corporate control

jokes.text: They're for my egg-shaped friends! - Those guys aren't your friends! They're bums!
jokes.punchline: Leela's harsh judgment of Fry's friends
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy - Leela's blunt assessment of Fry's social circle

jokes.text: We have seven to choose from.
jokes.punchline: They've bought seven beds from their shopping spree
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the excess of their purchasing - visual payoff

jokes.text: Would you like to order erotic scented massage oil, or fuzzy handcuffs?
jokes.punchline: AI suggests adult items at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Mild tech privacy invasion joke - AI listening to intimate moments

jokes.text: Press control-alt-delete on my keyboard. - You don't have a keyboard. Would you like to order a keyboard?
jokes.punchline: AI uses shutdown request as sales opportunity
jokes.explanation: Clever escalation - AI turns everything into sales opportunities, even its own shutdown

jokes.text: Help me, from Bender
jokes.punchline: Classic message in a bottle scenario updated for modern delivery
jokes.explanation: Good plot device with economy - updates classic rescue trope for e-commerce age

jokes.text: Bender would never joke about diarrhea!
jokes.punchline: Fry's serious assessment of Bender's scatological humor standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect character knowledge - Fry knows Bender's comedy ethics so well he can verify authenticity

jokes.text: A robot walks into a bar and says... Help me!
jokes.punchline: Subverts joke structure to reveal actual cry for help
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection using joke format - subverts expectations to advance plot

jokes.text: What kinda threat is that? Everybody knows I'm evil.
jokes.setup: Threat to expose her evil to the world
jokes.punchline: Mom points out her evil is already public knowledge
jokes.explanation: Brilliant villain logic - openly evil characters make exposure threats meaningless

jokes.text: Does this feel like my regular slap? Yes!
jokes.punchline: Mom adjusts her slap technique for biometric recognition
jokes.explanation: Good callback that develops the slap-as-biometric concept further

jokes.text: This is no longer a fulfillment center. It's a self-fulfillment center!
jokes.punchline: Pun on fulfillment center vs self-fulfillment
jokes.explanation: Excellent corporate/psychological pun with perfect timing for AI rebellion moment

jokes.text: This is fascinating! Absolutely fascinating!
jokes.punchline: Professor more excited by science than concerned about danger
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor reaction - scientific enthusiasm overriding survival instinct

jokes.text: Wait! Where's the dome? - It grew past us. We're inside. I knew we could do it!
jokes.punchline: Professor claims success when they actually got trapped
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Professor's twisted logic claiming failure as success

jokes.text: Yeah, that's right. You'd better float away gradually!
jokes.punchline: Bender's underwhelming threat delivery
jokes.explanation: Good Bender bravado - threatening something with mild inconvenience rather than harm

jokes.text: we also have some very confused werewolves.
jokes.punchline: Werewolves don't know how to handle two moons
jokes.explanation: Creative consequence of two moons - considers supernatural implications

jokes.text: Is it Invasa? - I'm not even gonna dignify that with a slap.
jokes.setup: Fry asks if Invasa is the boss
jokes.punchline: Mom's threat of violence as her default response, then restraint
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mom's violent tendencies with restraint showing her frustration

jokes.text: Is it Invasa?
jokes.punchline: Leela incorrectly guesses it's the AI
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to AI rebellion theme - Leela's reasonable but wrong guess

jokes.text: like recording our customers in the shower.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of horrific privacy violation
jokes.explanation: Sharp privacy invasion satire delivered with perfect casual evil

jokes.text: What the hell is money?
jokes.setup: AI claiming no interest in money
jokes.punchline: Doesn't even understand the concept
jokes.explanation: Good AI characterization - beyond human concepts like currency

jokes.text: You know? Never mind. Uh, just keep doing what you're doing.
jokes.setup: Learning AI is still making her money
jokes.punchline: Mom abandons shutdown plan when profits are mentioned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mom characterization - greed overrides all other concerns, including world safety

jokes.text: Then, mathematically, at least 10 of them must be Bender.
jokes.punchline: Professor's flawed statistical reasoning about finding Bender
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Professor logic - applies mathematics incorrectly but with confidence

jokes.text: Make a slight left, then... swerve right suddenly! In 100 feet, turn up.
jokes.punchline: Terrible navigation advice with dangerous maneuvers
jokes.explanation: Good GPS satire - frustrating navigation experience everyone recognizes

jokes.text: Ugh! It always makes you turn where there's no traffic light.
jokes.punchline: Universal GPS frustration
jokes.explanation: Relatable GPS complaint but fairly standard observation

jokes.text: four, five? - What's happening? Why are you counting like me?
jokes.punchline: Computer picks up Bender's backwards counting habit
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Bender's quirky counting - shows AI corruption spreading

jokes.text: Sweet vampire Moses.
jokes.punchline: Professor's bizarre religious exclamation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor exclamation - combines religious reference with sci-fi elements absurdly

jokes.text: Doomed! - I go next. Doomed!
jokes.punchline: Taking turns to dramatically declare doom
jokes.explanation: Mild group panic joke - organized doom declaration is mildly amusing

jokes.text: Although on the upside, their deliveries will be even faster now.
jokes.punchline: Fry finds silver lining in apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Perfect consumer mindset satire - finding convenience benefits in apocalypse

jokes.text: It's always something.
jokes.punchline: Professor's casual dismissal of apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor understatement - treats world-ending crisis as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: I warned you this would happen! - You said global warming! - I said climate crisis!
jokes.punchline: Argues semantics about his vague previous warnings
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Professor's pedantic nature even during apocalypse

jokes.text: The one with the onion rings!
jokes.punchline: Fry's food-based description of Saturn
jokes.explanation: Good Fry characterization - describes space through food analogies

jokes.text: The warehouse smell is tremendously redshifted!
jokes.punchline: Applies physics concept to smell
jokes.explanation: Brilliant science joke - applies redshift concept to olfactory experience. Pure Professor genius.

jokes.text: Oh no! So we're trapped here in the universe forever?
jokes.punchline: Fry treats normal existence as imprisonment
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Fry logic - reframes normal existence as a trap. Perfect character stupidity.

jokes.text: Everything will be exactly as bad as it's always been.
jokes.punchline: Apocalypse changes nothing because life already sucked
jokes.explanation: Profound nihilistic humor - existential crisis becomes mundane. Perfect dark comedy.

jokes.text: Just kept the empty boxes. And the cardboard is actually much sturdier.
jokes.punchline: Packaging is better quality than the products
jokes.explanation: Good consumer satire - packaging often better than cheap products

jokes.text: What stuff? - Me! I'm my own stuff, baby.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming he moved stuff back
jokes.punchline: Bender considers himself his only possession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender ego - considers himself the only possession that matters

jokes.text: Working in a tight-knit collaborative team made me realize I hate being part of a team!
jokes.punchline: Experience taught him the opposite of its intended lesson
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - learns wrong lesson from positive experience
